steps.title: Calculate outer and inner radii
steps.content: The pipe has an outside diameter of 60 inches, so the outer radius $ R = 30 $ inches. The wall thickness is $ \frac{5}{8} $ inches, so the inner radius $ r = 30 - \frac{5}{8} = 29.375 $ inches. The height $ h = 144 $ inches.

steps.title: Compute volume using difference of squares
steps.content: Factor the volume formula as $ \pi h (R - r)(R + r) $. $ R - r = 0.625 $, $ R + r = 59.375 $. Multiply: $ 0.625 \times 59.375 = 37.109375 $. Multiply by $ h $: $ 144 \times 37.109375 = 5343.75 $. $ \pi \times 5343.75 \approx 3.1416 \times 5343.75 $.

steps.title: Approximate total volume
steps.content: Calculate $ 3.1416 \times 5343.75 $: Approximate $ 3.14 \times 5343.75 \approx 16,787.88 $. The closest choice is $ D $. That matches choice D.
